mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate keeps discount rates compressed and supports equity valuation multiples, so a low or falling FEDFUNDS level predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above their unconditional mean when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25%, or if the sign of the conditional return is negative in a majority of low-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and slower earnings expectations, so a monthly increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus the prior month
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad demand acceleration, which supports revenues and cyclical sentiment and therefore produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST were both available and elevated in the 2014-07 snapshot
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint-high regime for RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform each series used alone, or if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of joint-expansion observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger real activity and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and supports equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive 3-month changes in INDPRO are not followed by above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the conditional effect reverses in multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts break above a recent baseline, the market interprets it as a durable demand impulse for construction, credit, and durable goods, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Rejected if breakout months in HOUST are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average conditional return is not statistically above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPI inflation rate without a matching policy-rate response tightens real financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, so inflation acceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is unchanged
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value exceeded the revised value in 2014-06
failure_condition: Rejected if inflation-acceleration episodes with flat FEDFUNDS do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value over CPI alone.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate marks a labor-market deterioration that weakens earnings expectations and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 139004.0 thousand and UNRATE was 6.2% in 2014-07
failure_condition: Rejected if joint weak-labor observations do not show negative 60-day SPX returns more frequently than positive returns, or if the combined signal is not stronger than either labor series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a stable-rate environment, stronger GDP growth confirms resilient final demand and supports earnings revisions, so an upward GDP print predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1% and GDPC1 growth is positive versus the prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 15985.7 in 2014-04 and FEDFUNDS was 0.09 in 2014-07
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with low policy rates
failure_condition: Rejected if positive GDP growth under low FEDFUNDS does not precede above-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the GDP effect vanishes once the low-rate condition is enforced.
